findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `acli.py:76` (`from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf`) to the explicit edit-set in the 'Surviving intra-adapter references to repoint' section — after the move this dotted import must become `from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.adf import text_to_adf`.
evidence: acli.py:76: `from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf as _text_to_adf  # canonical location`
Plan names `acli_cli_ops.py:36` explicitly but does not list `acli.py:76`
acli.py is one of the eight modules being physically moved, so its dotted import must also be updated to `from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.adf import`
S4 ticket scope explicitly excludes acli.py (it is adapter-internal: 'acli.py:145 (adapter-internal client construction — stays in the adapter, moves in S5)')
findings.finding: acli.py:76 (`from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf`) is a surviving dotted-import reference site that the plan's edit-set omits. Since acli.py is one of the eight files being moved to adapters/jira/, after the move this import will refer to a non-existent root-level path and will fail at runtime.
findings.location: Plan section 'Edit-set' / 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references to repoint'
scenarios: Implementer follows the plan's explicit edit-set, moves acli.py to adapters/jira/, but leaves acli.py:76 unchanged — the `from rebar_reconciler.adf import` fails at module load time because rebar_reconciler.adf no longer exists at the root.

The plan's structural gate ('grep finds zero references to old root paths in src/') would catch this, but only if the implementer knows to include acli.py:76 in the grep scan — the omission from the edit-set creates an implementation gap.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet under 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references to repoint': `acli.py:76` — `from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf as _text_to_adf` (moves with the `acli*` cluster; becomes an intra-adapter import, same as `acli_cli_ops.py:36`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the `jira_fields.py` edit-set entry to cover all three import lines (`:18` for `adf` AND `:19–24` for `comment_limits`), repointing both to `rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.adf` and `rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.comment_limits` respectively.
evidence: jira_fields.py:18: `from rebar_reconciler.adf import fit_text_to_adf_limit` (named in plan)
jira_fields.py:19-24: `from rebar_reconciler.comment_limits import _JIRA_COMMENT_MAX_CHARS` and `from rebar_reconciler.comment_limits import truncate_comment_body` (not named in plan)
comment_limits.py is one of the eight modules being moved to adapters/jira/
jira_fields.py is NOT in S4's scope — it is an existing adapters/jira/ file, not a core rewire site
findings.finding: `jira_fields.py` (already in `adapters/jira/`) imports `from rebar_reconciler.comment_limits import` at lines 19–24, in addition to the `adf` import at line 18 the plan names. The plan's edit-set for `jira_fields.py` lists only `:18` but comment_limits must also be repointed to `rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.comment_limits` after the move, or the module fails to import.
findings.location: Plan section 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references to repoint', `jira_fields.py:18` bullet
scenarios: Implementer updates jira_fields.py:18 (adf import) as stated but overlooks lines 19-24 (comment_limits imports), leaving `from rebar_reconciler.comment_limits import` pointing to the now-missing root-level module — causing ImportError on any code path through jira_fields.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the bullet `jira_fields.py:18` to `jira_fields.py:18–24` and note: 'repoint both `from rebar_reconciler.adf import fit_text_to_adf_limit` (line 18) and `from rebar_reconciler.comment_limits import …` (lines 19–24) to `rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.adf` / `rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.comment_limits`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `acli.py:76` (`from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf`) to the enumerated surviving dotted references and include its repoint (`from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.adf import …`) in the atomic change set.
evidence: Grep result: `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli.py:76: from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf as _text_to_adf  # canonical location`
Plan enumeration lists only three dotted refs to repoint: `jira_fields.py:18`, `inbound_fields.py:36`, `acli_cli_ops.py:36`
`acli.py` is one of the eight files being physically moved into `adapters/jira/`; after the move it will co-reside with `adf.py` but the import still names the old dotted path
findings.finding: `acli.py:76` contains `from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf as _text_to_adf` — a dotted import that must be repointed to `rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.adf` after the move — but it is absent from the plan's enumerated 'surviving dotted/sibling references' list (`jira_fields.py:18`, `inbound_fields.py:36`, `acli_cli_ops.py:36`).
findings.location: Context/Problem — 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references to repoint'; Scope section; acli.py:76
scenarios: After the move, `acli.py` at `adapters/jira/acli.py` imports `from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf` — but `adf.py` no longer exists at the package root, causing an ImportError at module load time.

The structural grep gate ('zero references to old root paths') would catch this, but the plan's implementation step silently misses it, leaving a broken import for the implementer to discover only at test run.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references to repoint' bullet: `acli.py:76` — `from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf as _text_to_adf` → `from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.adf import …` (both files now co-located under `adapters/jira/`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `outbound_fields.py:69` (`lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adf.py')`) to the reference update set and repoint it to `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adapters/jira/adf.py')` (or convert to a direct intra-package import after co-location).
evidence: Grep result: `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_fields.py:69: _AdfModule = lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adf.py')`
Plan explains that `lazy_load(key, filename)` resolves `path = _PACKAGE_DIR / filename` where `_PACKAGE_DIR` is always the package root, regardless of which moved module calls it

Plan's loader-fix section only mentions calling `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adapters/jira/adf.py')` for the three location-pinned modules at the external call-sites (loader registration), not for `outbound_fields.py`'s own internal lazy_load call
findings.finding: `outbound_fields.py:69` contains `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adf.py')` — a root-relative filename passed to the loader. Since `outbound_fields.py` is one of the eight files being moved AND `adf.py` is also being moved, after relocation `_PACKAGE_DIR / 'adf.py'` no longer exists. This internal lazy_load call is not listed in the plan's reference update set.
scenarios: After the move, `outbound_fields.py` at `adapters/jira/outbound_fields.py` calls `lazy_load('rebar_reconciler.adf', 'adf.py')`, which resolves to `_PACKAGE_DIR / 'adf.py'` — a file that no longer exists at the root, causing FileNotFoundError/ImportError at runtime.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Loader section or the 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references' section, add: `outbound_fields.py:69` — `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adf.py')` → `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adapters/jira/adf.py')`, since both files will reside in `adapters/jira/` after the move.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `jira_fields.py:19-23` (`from rebar_reconciler.comment_limits import …`) to the enumerated surviving dotted references and repoint both imports to `from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.comment_limits import …` in the atomic change.
evidence: Grep result: `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/jira_fields.py:19: from rebar_reconciler.comment_limits import (  # shared send/diff truncation`
Grep result: `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/jira_fields.py:22: from rebar_reconciler.comment_limits import (`

Plan's surviving-reference enumeration for jira_fields.py reads: 'jira_fields.py:18 — from rebar_reconciler.adf import fit_text_to_adf_limit → from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.adf import …' — stops there, no mention of comment_limits
findings.finding: `jira_fields.py` lines 19–23 contain two imports from `rebar_reconciler.comment_limits` (`_JIRA_COMMENT_MAX_CHARS` and `truncate_comment_body`). After the move, `comment_limits.py` will be at `adapters/jira/comment_limits.py`. Only the `jira_fields.py:18` (adf) import is enumerated for repointing; the `comment_limits` imports on lines 19–23 are not listed.
findings.location: Context/Problem — 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references to repoint'; Scope section; jira_fields.py:19-23
scenarios: After `comment_limits.py` moves to `adapters/jira/`, `jira_fields.py` continues to import `from rebar_reconciler.comment_limits import …`. If `comment_limits.py` is no longer at the root (per AC #1: 'no source file remains at their old root paths'), this import fails at module load time.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the `jira_fields.py:18` bullet to cover lines 19–23 as well: both `from rebar_reconciler.comment_limits import _JIRA_COMMENT_MAX_CHARS` and `from rebar_reconciler.comment_limits import truncate_comment_body` → `from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.comment_limits import …`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove the false `__main__._load_sibling_keyed` / `spec_from_file_location` update claim and replace it with the correct sites: `outbound_fields.py:69`, and the S4-residual sites in `outbound_comments.py` / `inbound_translate.py` / `reconcile_check.py` that survive after S4 lands.
evidence: Grep of `__main__.py` for 'adf': no matches
Grep of `__main__.py` for 'outbound_fields': no matches
`__main__._load_sibling_keyed` is only used for `mode.py` and `_advisory_lock.py` (lines 516, 534) — neither is being moved
The actual sites loading adf/outbound_fields/comment_limits dynamically are in `outbound_comments.py`, `inbound_translate.py`, `outbound_fields.py`, `reconcile_check.py` — not `__main__`
findings.finding: The plan claims 'Update `__main__._load_sibling_keyed` and the `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(...)` sites for these three [location-pinned modules] the same way', but `__main__.py` contains zero references to `adf`, `outbound_fields`, or `comment_limits` — the asserted update sites do not exist.
findings.location: Context/Problem — Loader section: 'Update `__main__._load_sibling_keyed` and the `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(...)` sites for these three the same way'
scenarios: An implementer following the plan will search `__main__.py` for `_load_sibling_keyed` calls for the three modules and find none, introducing confusion about which files actually need loader updates and risking that the real update sites (outbound_comments.py:84,100; inbound_translate.py:323; outbound_fields.py:69; reconcile_check.py:70,75) are overlooked.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `acli.py:76` (`from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf`) to the named edit-set alongside `acli_cli_ops.py:36` — both intra-cluster imports need repointing to `rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.adf` after the move.
evidence: grep result: `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli.py:76: from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf as _text_to_adf  # canonical location`
Plan names `acli_cli_ops.py:36 — from rebar_reconciler.adf import …` as a named edit target but omits the identical line in acli.py:76
Both files are in the moved cluster; the intra-cluster import must be repointed
findings.finding: acli.py:76 contains `from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf as _text_to_adf` — an identical dotted import to acli_cli_ops.py:36 which is named in the plan — but acli.py:76 is NOT listed as an edit target. After acli.py moves to adapters/jira/, this self-referential old-root import must be repointed to `from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.adf import ...`.
findings.location: Edit-set / Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references section; src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli.py:76
scenarios: After the move, acli.py imports adf from the old root path which no longer exists, causing ImportError at runtime and during the test suite.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the category-2 path-constant rewrite to include `test_outbound_description_length_convergence.py:31`, `test_inbound_differ.py:26`, and `test_outbound_comment_length_convergence.py:145`, which use `ENGINE / 'adf.py'` and `REPO_ROOT/.../comment_limits.py` absolute path constants pointing to the old root.
evidence: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_outbound_description_length_convergence.py:31: ADF_PATH = ENGINE / 'adf.py'`
`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_inbound_differ.py:26: ADF_PATH = ENGINE / 'adf.py'`
`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_outbound_comment_length_convergence.py:145: REPO_ROOT / 'src' / 'rebar' / '_engine' / 'rebar_reconciler' / 'comment_limits.py'`
Plan's category-2 description covers `SCRIPTS_DIR/… 'acli.py'`-style path constants but does not call out these ENGINE-relative path constants explicitly
These files use a different base variable (ENGINE vs SCRIPTS_DIR) but are semantically identical category-2 targets
findings.finding: Two additional test files use absolute `ENGINE / 'adf.py'` path constants (not `SCRIPTS_DIR`-style) that also point to the old root location and are not in the plan's category-2 edit list: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_outbound_description_length_convergence.py:31` (`ADF_PATH = ENGINE / 'adf.py'`) and `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_inbound_differ.py:26` (`ADF_PATH = ENGINE / 'adf.py'`). Similarly, `test_outbound_comment_length_convergence.py:145` hardcodes an absolute path to `comment_limits.py` at the old root.
scenarios: These tests load adf/comment_limits by absolute path pointing at the old root; after the move the path resolves to a non-existent file and the test fails with a spec-load assertion error.
findings.suggested_fix: Update `ADF_PATH = ENGINE / 'adapters/jira/adf.py'` (and similarly for `comment_limits.py`) in these three files as part of the category-2 rewrite.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC or sub-bullet to AC[3] or AC[5] that requires the 'Teeth' check: stage one category-2 path constant at the old path, confirm the test suite goes RED, then restore — so sensitivity of the suite to path regressions is a gated criterion, not an optional step.
evidence: "Teeth: leave one category-2 path constant at the old path and confirm that test errors."
No AC references this step or requires evidence that the test suite is sensitive to category-2 path regressions.
findings.finding: The Testing section describes a 'Teeth' verification step ('leave one category-2 path constant at the old path and confirm that test errors') as an explicitly named deliverable, but no Acceptance Criterion covers it — it is uncovered described work.
findings.location: Testing section — 'Teeth' bullet; AC list (no corresponding criterion)
scenarios: Implementer skips the 'Teeth' check because no AC gates on it, leaving the regression-sensitivity of the test suite unverified.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC[5]: 'Prior to the final green run, one category-2 path constant is temporarily left at the old path and the suite is confirmed to error (teeth check), then the constant is restored before the final run.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a doc update (AGENTS.md loader section, an inline docstring in `_loader.py`, or an addendum to ADR 0035) capturing the relative-subpath convention for location-pinned sub-package modules, so future adapter contributors know to follow it.
evidence: 'teach the loader via relative subpaths' — plan describes the mechanism but mentions no doc/ADR update
Plan references ADR 0035 §(c) and Decision 4 but does not say to add to ADR 0035 or any design doc
ABSENCE: no 'update AGENTS.md' or 'update ADR 0035' task in Scope or AC
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new loader contract (relative subpaths passed to `lazy_load` for location-pinned modules in sub-packages) but does not specify any update to AGENTS.md, ADR 0035, or inline loader documentation — leaving future contributors without guidance on the pattern for adding new location-pinned adapter modules.
findings.location: Approach section / loader update description; Scope section
scenarios: A future developer adds a new location-pinned module in a different adapter sub-package and uses an absolute path (the old pattern), breaking the loader — because the relative-subpath convention was never documented.
A code reviewer sees `lazy_load(key, 'adapters/jira/adf.py')` and doesn't know if it's intentional or a bug, because no doc captures this pattern.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a task to Scope: 'Update `_loader.py` module docstring and AGENTS.md loader section to document the relative-subpath convention for location-pinned modules in adapter sub-packages.' Add a corresponding AC: 'A comment or doc in `_loader.py` explains that sub-package modules are registered via relative subpaths (e.g., `adapters/jira/adf.py`), distinguishing them from root-level modules.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a task (Scope or AC) to update ADR 0035's migration status section (or its changelog) to record that the final Jira modules have been relocated and the adapter boundary migration is complete.
evidence: Plan invokes ADR 0035 §(c), §1, §2, and Decision 4 as authoritative, implying the ADR is a living reference
'This is the last story' — implies ADR 0035's migration status description becomes stale after this lands
ABSENCE: no 'update ADR 0035 status' or 'mark migration complete' task anywhere in the plan
findings.finding: ADR 0035 presumably describes the migration seam and possibly names the old root locations as the current/transitional state; the plan does not include a task to update ADR 0035 (or any changelog/status section) to reflect that the migration is now complete, potentially leaving the ADR with stale 'not yet moved' language.
findings.location: Context / Problem section: 'Per ADR 0035 §(c) the seam is adapters/jira/'
scenarios: A new team member reads ADR 0035 post-merge and sees language describing acli*/adf/outbound_fields/comment_limits as 'still at package root' — a stale description that contradicts the actual codebase.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the ambiguous 'repoint or drop' with a stated default: if any S4-residual adf/comment_limits/outbound_fields loader ref survives in outbound_comments.py / inbound_translate.py / reconcile_check.py, **repoint** it to the new adapters/jira/ path (consistent with the 'no behavior changes' constraint); 'drop' is only permissible when S4's own AC explicitly records the ref as dead.
evidence: "Any outbound_comments.py / inbound_translate.py / reconcile_check.py adf/comment_limits loader refs **not already eliminated by S4** — grep-confirmed at S5 time; repoint or drop."
Approach section: 'No behavior changes — a pure relocation + rebind.'
If 'drop' is chosen and the ref was still live, that is a behavior change contradicting the stated constraint.
findings.finding: The plan defers a real design choice to the executor with the phrase 'repoint or drop' for S4-residual loader refs in outbound_comments.py / inbound_translate.py / reconcile_check.py, yet elsewhere the plan mandates 'no behavior changes'. 'Drop' could be a behavior change if any of those refs still serve a runtime purpose after S4 lands; the executor cannot know at plan time which action is correct, making this a placeholder rather than a decision.
findings.location: Context / Problem — 'Any outbound_comments.py / inbound_translate.py / reconcile_check.py adf/comment_limits loader refs not already eliminated by S4 — grep-confirmed at S5 time; repoint or drop.'
scenarios: Executor greps, finds a residual adf loader ref in reconcile_check.py post-S4, is uncertain whether to repoint it (safe) or drop it (potentially removes behavior), and must stop to ask — exactly the clarifying question E2 prohibits.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the phrase to: 'Any outbound_comments.py / inbound_translate.py / reconcile_check.py adf/comment_limits loader refs not already eliminated by S4 — grep-confirmed at S5 time; **repoint to adapters/jira/** (dropping a ref is a behavior change and is out of scope unless S4's AC explicitly records it as dead).'}

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out plan to the Approach section — e.g., 'If the suite fails or a missed consumer is discovered post-merge, revert the PR via `git revert <sha>`; re-export shims are explicitly prohibited so partial rollback is not available' — so the irreversibility is a stated, justified choice with a named recovery path.
evidence: "One atomic change (the constraints forbid a partial move)."
"No re-export shim remains at any old path" (AC[1]).
No mention of 'git revert', 'back-out branch', or any rollback procedure anywhere in the plan.
The grep gate detects missed consumers pre-merge, but no recovery path is described for post-merge discovery.
findings.finding: The plan performs an atomic, irreversible destructive operation (removing eight modules from the package root with no re-export shims, breaking all unconverted consumers instantly) but does not state any rollback or back-out mechanism if the suite fails after partial application or if a missed consumer is discovered post-merge.
scenarios: A missed consumer reference is discovered post-merge in a downstream repo or CI environment not covered by the local grep; with no re-export shim and no rollback plan, the fix requires an emergency follow-up PR.
The full suite fails partway through the atomic change and the implementer has no described procedure for reverting to a known-good state.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to Approach: 'Back-out: if any missed consumer is discovered post-merge, revert the entire commit (`git revert <sha>`) — partial rollback is infeasible by design (no shims), so the whole atomic change must be re-landed once the gap is fixed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the 5 unenumerated category-3 test consumers (`test_error_taxonomy.py:25`, `test_config_jira_wiring.py:20`, `test_config_file_wiring.py:21`, `test_acli_subprocess_timeout.py:25`, `test_comment_pagination.py:27`) to the category-3 rewrite set in the Edit-set section so they are repointed to `rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.*` in the atomic change.

`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config_jira_wiring.py:20: from rebar_reconciler.acli_subprocess import resolve_jira_settings`
`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config_file_wiring.py:21: from rebar_reconciler import acli_cli_ops, acli_subprocess`
`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_acli_subprocess_timeout.py:25: from rebar_reconciler import acli_cli_ops, acli_subprocess`
`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_comment_pagination.py:27: from rebar_reconciler import acli_cli_ops, acli_subprocess`

Plan's category-3 description covers `from rebar_reconciler.acli… / .adf / .outbound_fields` but the structural grep gate targets `src/` and `tests/`, and AC3 says 'deduped category-2/3 test targets reference the new adapters.jira.* paths' — these 5 files are not enumerated
findings.finding: Several test files use direct dotted imports of the moved modules at the OLD root package path and are not in the plan's category-3 edit set: `test_error_taxonomy.py:25` (`from rebar_reconciler.acli import RetryExhaustedError`), `test_config_jira_wiring.py:20` (`from rebar_reconciler.acli_subprocess import resolve_jira_settings`), `test_config_file_wiring.py:21` (`from rebar_reconciler import acli_cli_ops, acli_subprocess`), `test_acli_subprocess_timeout.py:25` (`from rebar_reconciler import acli_cli_ops, acli_subprocess`), and `test_comment_pagination.py:27` (`from rebar_reconciler import acli_cli_ops, acli_subprocess`). After the move these imports resolve nothing.
scenarios: After the move, `from rebar_reconciler.acli import ...` raises ImportError because acli.py no longer lives at the package root, breaking these tests.
The structural grep gate (`grep` for old root paths in `tests/`) would catch these, but the plan's implementation step doesn't list them as files requiring edits, risking them being overlooked.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `outbound_comments.py` as a definite (not conditional) named repoint target in the Scope and AC — either confirm S4 fully eliminates all its `adf`/`comment_limits` loader refs (in which case state that explicitly and note S5 has nothing to do) or list it alongside `inbound_fields.py:36` as an S5 repoint target, to satisfy the epic's AC that names both files.
evidence: Epic AC: 'the two non-moved root modules that reference them by sibling filename (inbound_fields.py, outbound_comments.py) are repointed' — both named as S5 deliverables.
Leaf Scope bullet: 'Repoint every surviving dotted/sibling reference (incl. jira_fields.py:18, inbound_fields.py:36, acli_cli_ops.py:36)' — outbound_comments.py is absent from the explicit list.

S4 scope does include dropping outbound_comments.py lazy_load calls (:84,:100), but if any survive S4, the leaf's AC does not firmly commit to handling them.
findings.finding: The epic's S5 AC explicitly names both `inbound_fields.py` and `outbound_comments.py` as 'the two non-moved root modules that reference them by sibling filename' that must be repointed in S5. The leaf's Scope section lists `inbound_fields.py:36` as a definite named repoint target but treats `outbound_comments.py` as conditional on S4's output ('Any outbound_comments.py … refs not already eliminated by S4 — grep-confirmed at S5 time; repoint or drop'), and omits it from the explicit Scope bullet listing named repoint targets. If S4 does not fully eliminate the `outbound_comments.py` adf/comment_limits lazy_load references, this gap remains unaddressed in S5, violating the parent's AC.
findings.location: Leaf Scope section ('Repoint every surviving dotted/sibling reference') and leaf Context section ('Any outbound_comments.py / inbound_translate.py / reconcile_check.py adf/comment_limits loader refs not already eliminated by S4'); vs. epic AC bullet 4 ('the two non-moved root modules that reference them by sibling filename (inbound_fields.py, outbound_comments.py) are repointed')
scenarios: S4 lands but does not fully eliminate outbound_comments.py's adf/comment_limits loader references (e.g., a partial drop or a new reference introduced during S4 refactor). S5 proceeds without explicitly repointing outbound_comments.py, leaving a dangling sibling-path reference to the old root location. The epic's AC is unmet even though the leaf's own AC is satisfied.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope bullet, change 'Repoint every surviving dotted/sibling reference (incl. jira_fields.py:18, inbound_fields.py:36, acli_cli_ops.py:36)' to add a clause: 'if any outbound_comments.py adf/comment_limits loader refs survive S4, repoint them here (per epic AC); if S4 eliminates them entirely, confirm with grep and document as zero-work.' Then add a matching AC line: 'outbound_comments.py has no references to the old root paths of the moved modules — verified by grep (either S4 dropped them or S5 repoints them).'
